steps.title: Simplify the equations
steps.content:
The first equation is already $x+6y=-14$. Dividing the second equation by $5$ gives:
$$
x+6y=0
$$
Dividing the third equation by $2$ gives:
$$
x+6y=19.5
$$

steps.title: Compare the lines
steps.content:
All three equations have the same left side, so they represent lines with the same slope. Their constants are different:
$$
-14,\ 0,\ 19.5
$$
Thus the lines are distinct and parallel, so they have no common point.

steps.title: Count the solutions
steps.content: A system of distinct parallel lines has zero solutions. The answer is A.
